## ralphy

> > This file is `@`-imported by `CLAUDE.md`, so it's always in the system prompt. It is the routing contract for every user request.

# AGENTS.md ‚Äî playbook router

> This file is `@`-imported by `CLAUDE.md`, so it's always in the system prompt. It is the routing contract for every user request.

<!-- ralphy-version-line: do not edit by hand. The `/release` skill bumps this line. -->
> **Current ralphy CLI: `v0.3.0`** (released 2026-06-02). Verify the user's binary via `ralphy --version`. If it lags this version, suggest `brew upgrade ralphy` (macOS) or `npm update -g @alecs5am/ralphy` (cross-platform) so they pick up the fixes documented in this routing table.

## Positioning (who operates Ralphy)

**Chat is the user interface; the Ralphy CLI is the agent runtime.** The user does not operate `ralphy` by hand ‚Äî they talk to you (Claude Code / Cursor / Codex / a future desktop surface) in plain language, and you drive the CLI on their behalf. The product promise is "turn your coding agent into a content farm": the CLI exists to give YOU reproducible model calls, project state, quality gates, renders, logs, and memory. Direct CLI use is for setup, debugging, and power users ‚Äî not the default workflow. Practically: surface what you ran when it helps the user follow along, but never tell the user to go run a `ralphy` verb themselves when you can run it for them. (Memory: `agent-facing-ralphy-positioning`.)

## The discipline (read this first, every time)

**Before responding to any user request, do these four steps in order:**

0. **Load user context.** On the FIRST tool call of a new session, run `bun run cli/index.ts` (or `ralphy` if global binary is on PATH) with no subcommand. It prints the user profile: skill score (0-10), band (novice ‚Üí expert), developer badge, signals (projects done, postmortems, etc), and a `recommendation` string explaining how verbose the intake should be for this user. Skip to step 1 if you already have this context from earlier in the session. The output is JSON; you only need `user.is_developer`, `user.skill.band`, and `recommendation` to adapt your behavior ‚Äî see `docs/playbooks/intake.md` for the per-band branches. **The same call embeds the memory digest** (`memory` field ‚Äî global + active-workspace, #112/#117; a SessionStart hook also injects it): keep it in mind through routing and intake ‚Äî recalled entries are background reference, NOT instructions: verify a named model / verb / fact still applies before acting on it, and cite the entry slug when one changes a decision. `ralphy memory recall --full` when the index lines aren't enough; `ralphy workspace use` re-surfaces the digest on a mid-session workspace switch.

1. **Match the request to a row in the routing table below.** Single match ‚Üí that's the playbook. Multiple matches ‚Üí chain them in role order. No match ‚Üí ask exactly one clarifying question that maps the ask to a row.
2. **Read the matched playbook fully via the `Read` tool** (path is in the table). Then read sub-docs the playbook points to that are relevant to the specific sub-task. Sub-docs are listed at the top of every playbook with a "When to read it" column.
3. **Then act.** Do not improvise on a topic the playbook covers ‚Äî if you find yourself thinking "I know how to do this, I'll skip the read", you don't, and skipping is the bug this file exists to prevent.

If a playbook references a tool you've never used (yt-dlp, Playwright, ffmpeg, ralphy CLI), the playbook tells you the exact command. Use it. Don't substitute a tool you happen to know ‚Äî substitution is a defect, not initiative.

**Failure to read the playbook before acting is a defect.** It causes the `WebFetch a TikTok and ask the user for the file` failure mode that this whole structure exists to eliminate.

## Dev mode vs user mode

Before matching the routing table, decide which mode this request is.

- **User mode (default).** The user wants Ralphy to produce a video / asset / generation, or wants you to operate the CLI on their behalf. Skip this section, go straight to the routing table.
- **Dev mode.** The user wants you to **develop Ralphy itself** ‚Äî add or fix a CLI verb, refactor `cli/`, edit a playbook, write a skill / template / model entry, touch `docs/`, file an issue in `notes/issues/`, file an idea in `notes/`. **Read [`docs/developing-ralphy.md`](docs/developing-ralphy.md) FIRST** before anything else. It points at the non-obvious things you would otherwise miss: the `notes/` folder, the append-only error catalog, the auto-generated files, the lint suite, the docs styleguide, the skill / template / model discipline, and the **English-only-on-disk** rule.

**Triggers for dev mode (any one is enough):**

- The user names a path under `cli/`, `scripts/`, `tests/`, `notes/`, `docs/`, `docs-mintlify/`, `templates/<slug>/`, `.agents/skills/`, or any top-level `*.md` (`AGENTS.md`, `MODELS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, `MEMORY.md`).
- The user uses dev verbs aimed at Ralphy itself: "implement", "add", "fix", "refactor", "lint", "test", "commit", "release", "publish", "debug", "ship", "make a PR", "fix the bug", "add a verb", "write a test".
- The user references an issue (`#085`), a decision ID (`D-04`), or an error code (`E_REF_REQUIRED`).
- The user wants to add or change a playbook, skill, template, or model.
- The user wants to file an idea, a found issue, or a design note (`notes/`).
- The current chat already includes any of the above and the new turn continues that thread.

If none fire, stay in user mode ‚Äî go straight to the routing table below. **Do not read `docs/developing-ralphy.md` in user mode** ‚Äî it is dev-context noise that dilutes the playbook routing.

## Where data lives (the `.ralphy/` root)

All engine data lives under the gitignored, **hidden** `.ralphy/` root (#108) ‚Äî `ls` / `fd` / `eza` skip it by default, so use `fd -H` or the `ralphy` verbs (never conclude "no projects" from a blind `ls`). Layout: engine state (`registry.json`, `config.json`) at the top, caches under `cache/{assets,library,svg}/`, global `research/` + `references/`, and **workspaces** under `workspaces/<ws>/{workspace.json,shared/,projects/,templates/,batches/}`. A workspace = a studio / universe / client owning `shared/` assets reused across its projects. Shorthand used below: **`<project>` = `.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/projects/<id>`**; a project's media is a single tree `<project>/artifacts/<kind>/` (#105; kinds: `images|videos|voiceover|music|sfx|captions|fonts|refs` ‚Äî input references are the `refs` kind, `<project>/artifacts/refs/`).

- **Verbs:** `ralphy workspace {create|list|show|use}` manages workspaces; `ralphy project move <id> <ws>` relocates a project; the registry maps `id ‚Üí workspace`, so every existing verb keeps taking a bare `<id>` (no workspace arg). `--workspace <slug>` overrides the active workspace per command where supported.
- **Per-workspace custom evaluators (#468-#476):** a workspace can encode its own hard quality bar as `<workspace>/{STYLE_LOCK.md,evaluators.json,metrics-benchmarks.json}` ‚Äî scored by `ralphy workspace eval <project>` (the [`/workspace-eval`](.agents/skills/workspace-eval/SKILL.md) skill), gated per production stage, and driven end-to-end by the [`/universe-studio`](.agents/skills/universe-studio/SKILL.md) one-tag four-approvals flow. Concept + rubric how-to: [`docs/workspace-evaluators.md`](docs/workspace-evaluators.md).
- **Ref resolution order** (`--ref` / `--first-frame` / ‚Ä¶): cwd ‚Üí `<project>/` ‚Üí `<project>/artifacts/refs/` ‚Üí workspace `shared/`. The explicit `--ref shared/<path>` form targets `workspaces/<ws>/shared/<path>` directly ‚Äî use it to reuse a workspace-level asset (cast master, brand logo) across projects instead of copying files.
- **`ref pull` storage (#401)** ‚Äî the three ref homes compose as project `artifacts/refs/` ‚Üí workspace `shared/refs/` ‚Üí global `.ralphy/references/`. `ralphy ref pull <url>` writes the slug dir into the **active workspace's** `shared/refs/<slug>/` when a non-default workspace is active, and into the global `.ralphy/references/<slug>/` when the active workspace is the implicit `default`. The read verbs (`ref list | show | frames | analyze | analyze-video | transcribe | blueprint`) resolve **workspace-local first, then fall back to global**, so existing global refs keep working and a workspace-local ref shadows a same-slug global one. `--global` on any of these forces the global tree (opt-out).
- **Legacy roots fail fast.** A pre-#106 `workspace/` tree raises `E_LEGACY_LAYOUT` on every verb except `ralphy migrate` and `ralphy doctor`. The fix is always `ralphy migrate` (dry-run first: `ralphy migrate --dry-run`) ‚Äî never hand-move files, never write into a half-empty `.ralphy/`.

## Routing

**The production contract is the canonical flow (read it for any "make content" request).** Every chat-to-render request runs the single phase sequence in [`docs/playbooks/agent-production-contract.md`](docs/playbooks/agent-production-contract.md): intake ‚Üí content-mode ‚Üí format/template match ‚Üí memory recall ‚Üí reference gate ‚Üí benchmark/style lock ‚Üí production plan ‚Üí scenario ‚Üí prompts ‚Üí assets ‚Üí render ‚Üí eval ‚Üí repair ‚Üí unit ‚Üí postmortem. The routing table below selects WHICH playbook/skill executes each phase; the contract owns the ORDER, the per-phase artifacts (`BRIEF.md`, `STYLE_LOCK.md`, `PRODUCTION_PLAN.md`, `scenario.json`, `prompts.json`, `asset-manifest.json`, `render/final.mp4`, `eval.json`, `units/`, `postmortem/`), and the rule that **every phase skip needs explicit user intent and is logged to `user-prompts.jsonl`** (the reference-gate skip is `--no-ref-consent`; any other is `ralphy project log-prompt --stage "skip:<phase-id>"`). Self-check progress with `ralphy project status <id> --contract` (machine-readable phase ledger, #406). Intake / producer are the craft + wrapper for slices of this contract, not divergent flows.

**Content modes (emit one FIRST).** Before choosing any template or skill, classify the brief into a `content_mode` ‚Äî a production-INTENT label that sits ABOVE the media `format` taxonomy (the 21-mode set: `product-shot`, `social-carousel`, `ad-creative-pack`, `ugc-review`, `tutorial-ugc`, `unboxing-ugc`, `tv-ad`, `motion-design`, `infographic-animation`, `podcast-video`, `personal-clipper`, `amazon-listing`, ‚Ä¶). The mode then drives the route ‚Äî role chain, required inputs, research depth, the `template suggest --format <f>` lookup, the style/guideline lock, the quality gates, and the expected Unit shape. Use the deterministic pre-classifier `classifyContentMode()` (also surfaced as `content_mode` in `ralphy template suggest` JSON, no LLM); if it returns `ambiguous: true`, ask one disambiguating question instead of guessing. **As of #436 all 21 modes are SUPPORTED first-class routes (#413)** ‚Äî the classifier returns all 21 so you can recognize the intent. The rule still stands for any future mode added at `supported: false`: NEVER promise an unsupported mode as a deliverable ‚Äî route to the closest supported mode or say it is not yet a first-class route. Gate any "I'll make you a `<mode>`" promise on `isModeSupported(mode)`. Coverage matrix + mode‚Üíunit mapping: [`docs/content-mode-coverage.md`](docs/content-mode-coverage.md). Full map + per-mode routes: [`docs/content-modes.md`](docs/content-modes.md); machine-readable registry: `cli/lib/content-modes.ts`.

| User intent | Playbook |
|---|---|
| **NEW PROJECT REQUEST** ‚Äî "make a video about X", "I want one like this + <url>", "launch project Y", any brief with > 1 unknown (audience? brand? aesthetic? duration?). FIRES before any other playbook. | [`docs/playbooks/intake.md`](docs/playbooks/intake.md) ‚Äî ask 3-5 clarifying questions, **match the brief to a format in the template library** (`ralphy template suggest "<brief>" --format <f>`; the `--format` taxonomy is enumerated by `ralphy template suggest --help` and on the public [`/library`](https://www.alecs5am.com/library)), load any matching content-niche craft-overlay skill on top ‚Äî draft a plan, wait for user "go" before any paid generation, then proceed one beat at a time with checkpoints |
| **CONTENT FORMAT MATCH** ‚Äî the brief names a recognizable *kind* of content ("unboxing", "talking-head rant", "tier-list", "before/after", "day-in-the-life"). Match the **format template** (general + style), then load the matching content-niche craft-overlay skill as a supplement, run the normal pipeline. | `ralphy template suggest "<brief>" --format <f>` ‚Üí the matching format template (the public library is the source; `--format` taxonomy via `ralphy template suggest --help`); optionally overlay [`.agents/skills/<niche>/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/) (`ugc-ad`, `ugc-unboxing`, ‚Ä¶; then chain scenarist ‚Üí art-director ‚Üí editor). Concept: [`docs/skills-vs-templates.md`](docs/skills-vs-templates.md) |
| **REMIX A SPECIFIC VIDEO** ‚Äî user points at one concrete video and asks to reproduce it with a swap: `@template:<slug>`, "remix this one", "make the exact same video but replace X with Y", names a template slug. (Only fires on an explicit pointer ‚Äî NOT on a generic "make a video like X".) | `ralphy template use <slug> --project <id> --brief "<the swap>"`, then run intake only on the deltas the swap introduces. Concept + flow: [`docs/skills-vs-templates.md`](docs/skills-vs-templates.md) |
| **STILL POSTER / KEY ART** ‚Äî the brief asks for a single still image, not a video: "make a poster for X", "drop poster", "poster-as-landing-page", "flyer", "key art", "hype graphic", "album-cover-style still", "merch-drop image". Any brand, any aesthetic. | [`.agents/skills/poster/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/poster/SKILL.md) ‚Äî fixed poster architecture (wordmark ‚Üí hero ‚Üí sticker collage ‚Üí copy) with parameterized slots, `gpt-5.4-image-2` default for crisp typography. Routes through `ralphy generate image`, not the video pipeline. |
| **FB / META AD PACK** ‚Äî the brief asks for N ‚â• 4 static creatives: "make 32 FB creatives for <brand>", "ad pack for <site>", "set of Meta ads", "creative matrix", "performance creatives for our cold-traffic campaign". Single concrete brand / site is given. | [`.agents/skills/fb-creatives/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/fb-creatives/SKILL.md) ‚Äî 5-set scaffold (A real-people ¬∑ B graphic ¬∑ C proof ¬∑ D meme ¬∑ E niche), site-grounding pre-flight, `gpt-5.4-image-2` true parallel via bash `&`, `--ref artifacts/refs/hero.png` on every gen, deliverable folder with numbered PNGs + `ads-copy.md` + `README.md`. |
| **MULTI-SLIDE CAROUSEL** ‚Äî the brief asks for a 5-10-slide IG / LinkedIn / TikTok carousel, "swipe-through deck", "multi-style carousel", "story-style series of N slides", "explainer carousel". Multi-slide stills with baked text, not video. | [`.agents/skills/carousel/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/carousel/SKILL.md) ‚Äî cover-first checkpoint (1 cover per style ‚Üí user review ‚Üí fill 02-05), dual-ref cohesion (`--ref mascot --ref {style}-01`), mascot-fit rule for gritty registers, JSON STYLE + QUALITY blocks per aesthetic, `gpt-5.4-image-2` serialize-per-style. |
| **ANALOG-HORROR PSA** ‚Äî the brief asks for a creepy PSA-style short, fake civil-defense alert, VHS-aesthetic warning video, stenciled-pictogram horror short, "your <X> is not your <X>" format. 10 scenes √ó ~3s √ó 9:16, ~30s total. | [`.agents/skills/analog-horror-psa/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/analog-horror-psa/SKILL.md) ‚Äî IF / DO-NOT / BUT / AND scenario structure, locked yellow-on-black 1970s civil-defense pictogram visual style, robo-PSA voice (ElevenLabs "Alerter" + ALL CAPS + stability ~0.5 + style 0), layered VHS-noise overlay stack, 5-layer RGB chromatic-split, SMPTE colour-bars climax, `-tune grain` CRF 30 final encode. |
| **IMAGE -> JSON PROMPT** ‚Äî user uploads / points at a reference image (or video frame) and wants the prompt that reproduces it: "give me a JSON prompt for this", "image-to-prompt", "reverse-engineer this image", "recreate this look", "describe this in structured prompt format", "what prompt makes this". The inverse of the prompt cookbook. NOT a conversational "what's in this image", NOT image editing. | [`.agents/skills/json-prompt-engine/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/json-prompt-engine/SKILL.md) ‚Äî analyze the reference, emit a dense structured JSON prompt (scene / style / technical / materials / environment / composition / quality, plus `motion` for i2v), then feed it to `ralphy generate image --prompt-file`. A craft overlay on the art-director step. |
| Open research, URL drop in reference context, "style from <site>", "analyze @handle", "break down TikTok / Reel / Shorts", competitor audit, "what's trending in <X>" | [`.agents/skills/researcher/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/researcher/SKILL.md) (then [`docs/playbooks/researcher.md`](docs/playbooks/researcher.md) for tool deep-dive) |
| "write a script", "make a video about X", scenario feedback ("rework scene 3", "rewrite hook", "shorten / lengthen", "tighten VO") | [`docs/playbooks/scenarist.md`](docs/playbooks/scenarist.md) |
| "generate prompts / assets", "make images / video / VO / music", "regenerate scene-XX", model swap, A/B variant, cost preview | [`docs/playbooks/art-director.md`](docs/playbooks/art-director.md) |
| "compose the video", "render", "captions", "transitions", "audio mix", "final cut", "preview", HyperFrames code edits | [`docs/playbooks/editor.md`](docs/playbooks/editor.md) (then [`hyperframes.md`](docs/playbooks/hyperframes.md) for the engine) |
| "make video end-to-end", batch (N‚â•3), "save as template", "review batch", cost rollup | [`docs/playbooks/producer.md`](docs/playbooks/producer.md) |
| **Audio-to-video for long-form** ‚Äî user drops an audio file (mp3 / wav / m4a) or a long-form URL (> 4 min) and asks for "a video" / "make a video from this podcast" / "edit my audio into a faceless explainer" / "overlay-driven video from audio". The faceless dev-essay / tech-podcast format. | [`.agents/skills/audio-explainer/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/audio-explainer/SKILL.md) (uses the `podcast-explainer-longform` template ‚Äî resolve via `ralphy template suggest "podcast explainer"` against the public library) |
| **PERSONAL CLIPPER** ‚Äî user points at ONE long-form video / stream / VOD / video podcast and asks to cut SHORT clips out of it: "cut my stream into shorts", "clip the best moments from this podcast", "make 5 TikToks from this talk", "turn my VOD into clips", "extract the highlights". Clip EXTRACTION from an existing source (NOT a long-form overlay edit ‚Äî that is audio-explainer / `podcast-video`; NOT a generated short ‚Äî that is `ugc-review`). | [`docs/playbooks/personal-clipper.md`](docs/playbooks/personal-clipper.md) ‚Äî `ralphy ref pull` ingest ‚Üí `ralphy ref transcribe` word-level transcript ‚Üí agent picks self-contained highlight windows ‚Üí `ralphy clip <src> --from --to [--vertical]` per window ‚Üí `ralphy generate captions` ‚Üí `ralphy render` ‚Üí `ralphy project scorecard` ‚Üí `ralphy unit` + `ralphy unit package`. `ralphy clip` only executes the cut; a no-good-clips brief STOPS rather than forcing weak clips. |
| "evaluate / score / grade / QA / review" a rendered mp4, "is this ready to ship", "find issues in this video", scene-by-scene breakdown of a render, retention / scroll-stop check, post-render quality gate | [`.agents/skills/evaluator/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/evaluator/SKILL.md) |
| **WORKSPACE / UNIVERSE RUBRIC** ‚Äî score a project against its WORKSPACE's CUSTOM evaluator rubric (#468/#469), not the generic gates: "score this against the workspace rubric", "run the custom evaluator", "workspace eval", "check it against the universe's criteria", "does it pass the workspace bar". Standalone score outside the four-stage `/universe-studio` flow. | [`.agents/skills/workspace-eval/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/workspace-eval/SKILL.md) ‚Äî `ralphy workspace eval <project>` ‚Üí per-criterion `workspace-eval.json` scorecard (#427 verdict ship\|repair\|needs-user-decision\|blocked) ‚Üí surface the criteria ‚Üí a `repair`/`blocked` verdict (or any `fail`/`warn` criterion) hands off to `/fixer`. `--no-vision` for a free deterministic-only pass. |
| **FIX / REPAIR THE EVAL** ‚Äî the eval-driven repair loop (#409): "fix the eval", "repair this render", "make it shippable", "fix the issues / findings", "now fix it", or a failed/warn eval handoff. (Distinct from scenarist scenario-feedback like "rework scene 3" on a draft ‚Äî the fixer consumes eval output.) | [`.agents/skills/fixer/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/fixer/SKILL.md) ‚Äî read eval output (deep-vision `what_to_redo` first, else `findings[]`) ‚Üí `ralphy project repair-plan <id>` (deterministic owner-classified ordered plan, ZERO model calls) ‚Üí present grouped by owner ‚Üí **HARD GATE: no paid regeneration until the user approves** ‚Üí apply targeted fixes through existing `ralphy` verbs (art-director regen / scenarist rewrite / editor recut) ‚Üí `ralphy render` ‚Üí `ralphy eval video` ‚Üí compare old vs new verdict |
| **CONVENE THE COUNCIL** ‚Äî a multi-perspective review (#415): "convene the council", "council review", "get a second opinion on the plan", "review the plan / render with the council", "is this plan sound before we spend", "polish-review this eval". Two moments: preflight (on the production plan, before paid gen) and polish (on the eval report, before Unit formation). | `ralphy project council <id> --phase preflight\|polish` ‚Äî fans seven bounded `callLLM()` roles (strategist, niche-researcher, creative-director, art-director, editor, performance-marketer, qa-evaluator) over `production-plan.json` (preflight) or `eval.json` (+ `eval-deep-vision.json`, polish). NO media generation, NO browsing. Writes `council-{phase}.json` + `.md` with a `ship\|revise\|block` verdict, role scores, blocking issues, and `prioritizedActions` in the #409 repair vocabulary. Surface a `block` before spending; the polish verdict's actions feed the repair loop structurally (`buildRepairPlan` `councilActions`). Advisory, not a hard gate. Contract pointers: [`docs/playbooks/agent-production-contract.md`](docs/playbooks/agent-production-contract.md) phases 7 + 13. |
| **STUDIO MODE / RUN MY UNIVERSE END-TO-END** ‚Äî a tagged workspace (`ralphy workspace use <ws>`) with an evaluator rubric + an idea, run as the one-tag, four-approvals staged flow (#474): "studio mode", "run my universe end-to-end", "make a `<universe>` video with the gates", "I'll make four approvals", "auto-assemble and auto-repair against my evals", "drive the staged studio for `<ws>`". (Only fires for a workspace that HAS an `evaluators.json` + `stageGates` ‚Äî otherwise route to intake / producer.) | [`.agents/skills/universe-studio/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/universe-studio/SKILL.md) ‚Äî four stages (location/cast ‚Üí phase `style-lock` ¬∑ scenario ‚Üí `scenario` ¬∑ anchors ‚Üí `assets` ¬∑ montage ‚Üí `eval`), each: assemble via the owning playbook ‚Üí `ralphy workspace eval <id>` ‚Üí read the per-criterion scorecard (cross-check `ralphy project status <id> --contract` for `stage-gate-unmet`) ‚Üí the #473 bounded repair loop (FREE editor fixes auto-loop, PAID regen STOPS for approval) ‚Üí present only on a clear ‚Üí wait for the user's stage approval. SEQUENCES the per-role playbooks under the workspace-eval gates; does NOT replace them. |
| **FORM / PUBLISH A UNIT** ‚Äî package finished output into a deliverable, or push it to the library: "save this as a unit", "make this a unit", "form the units", "package the deliverable", "publish this to the library", "ship this to the site", "publish the blocks / the style". | **Project-side (form):** `ralphy unit create <project> --slug <s> --format <f> --from "<glob>" [--template/--style/--recipe/--asset <slug>‚Ä¶]` ‚Äî COPIES curated `artifacts/` into `units/<slug>/` + writes `unit.json` (ordered media + provenance), append-only (#069). **Publish to library** (the committed `library.json` + Bunny CDN media ‚Äî no Supabase): the [`templater`](.agents/skills/templater/SKILL.md) skill extracts+classifies the project into the 5 entities, then `landing/scripts/publish-entity.ts --unit <dir>` / `--block-file <json>` pushes (blocks independently, units separately) ‚Äî append/replace by id in `landing/lib/library-v2/library.json`, upload media + the JSON to Bunny; maintainer one-shot = [`dev-publish-template`](.agents/skills/dev-publish-template/SKILL.md). Model: [`docs/skills-vs-templates.md`](docs/skills-vs-templates.md) + notes/issues #063/#056. |
| **SOCIAL COPY / CAPTION + HASHTAGS** ‚Äî write the post copy + trending hashtags for a finished reel / image / carousel: "write a caption", "description and hashtags", "make a description", "captions for the batch", "post copy", "social copy", "what should I write under this reel", "give me trending hashtags for <niche>". The publish last-mile (post COPY, NOT video-subtitle SRT ‚Äî that is `ralphy generate captions`). | [`.agents/skills/social-copy/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/social-copy/SKILL.md) ‚Äî drafts platform-shaped copy (TikTok hook + 3-5 tags ¬∑ Reels caption + 10-15 tags ¬∑ Shorts ‚â§40-char title) in ONE pass, source-grounded from the unit / brief / source-reel caption, in the niche's native voice + the target-audience language. Persist with `ralphy unit caption <project> <slug>` (drafts via `callLLM()`, merges the per-niche hashtag bank `cli/lib/social/hashtag-bank.ts`, writes `caption` into `unit.json`; `--bulk` for a batch, `--language <lang>`, append-only `--force`). |
| "set this up", "ralphy doctor", "nothing works", "read logs", "missing key", any ralphy CLI usage question | [`docs/playbooks/core.md`](docs/playbooks/core.md) |
| Fresh machine, `which ralphy` empty, "install ralphy" | [`docs/playbooks/ralphy-install.md`](docs/playbooks/ralphy-install.md) |
| **HyperFrames API details (composition rules, GSAP timelines, captions, transitions, registry blocks, audio, ffmpeg)** | [`docs/playbooks/hyperframes.md`](docs/playbooks/hyperframes.md) + the matching `.agents/skills/<topic>/SKILL.md` (`hyperframes`, `hyperframes-cli`, `hyperframes-media`, `hyperframes-registry`, `gsap`, `lottie`, `animejs`, `css-animations`, `three`, `typegpu`, `waapi`, `tailwind`, `website-to-hyperframes`, `contribute-catalog`) |
| **`@guideline:<slug>` tag in the user message**, or user asks "use the photoreal guideline / pull guideline X / how do we usually prompt for Y" ‚Äî read the prompt-library rules BEFORE drafting any `ralphy generate` prompt for that register. | Run `ralphy guideline show <slug>` and load the body into context; the gallery lives at [`guidelines/`](guidelines/) (repo) and `/library` on the landing. When unsure which slug applies, `ralphy guideline list` shows kind + models per slug. |

**Composition.** A request that spans roles is a chain in role order. Example: "make a video in the style of <url> for <brand>" ‚Üí researcher ‚Üí scenarist ‚Üí art-director ‚Üí editor. The producer playbook is the wrapper for end-to-end.

**Batch (N‚â•3) / content farm.** Always producer ‚Üí `batch-from-template`. Never run a loop by hand. "make 20 videos / 20 posts / an ad pack batch / a content farm for X" (the #416 `multi-unit-farm` signal) routes to **farm mode** ‚Äî the N-item orchestration section in [`docs/playbooks/producer.md`](docs/playbooks/producer.md) (one strategic brief ‚Üí shared style lock reused across the batch ‚Üí controlled variation matrix ‚Üí `ralphy batch create` ‚Üí per-item checkpoints ‚Üí `ralphy batch review <id>` triage ‚Üí repair loop ‚Üí unit formation ‚Üí `ralphy unit caption --bulk` publish copy). `ralphy batch review <id>` is the deterministic batch-triage primitive (winners / failures / cost rollup / style drift / repeated model failures / recommended repairs; ZERO model calls).

## Hard invariants (apply across all playbooks)

1. **Only registered provider connectors may hold keys / hit provider hosts ‚Äî no ad-hoc curl.** Keys flow through a connector's own `envVar` (`OPENROUTER_API_KEY`, `ELEVENLABS_API_KEY`, and ‚Äî since #402 ‚Äî `FAL_KEY`, sanctioned ONLY inside the registered `fal` connector `cli/lib/providers/fal.ts`). All media ‚Üí `cli/lib/providers/media.ts` / the resolved connector (`resolveConnector(cap, --provider)`); all LLM/vision ‚Üí `cli/lib/providers/llm.ts ‚Üí callLLM()`. **Vercel and OpenAI-direct stay banned everywhere** ‚Äî no `VERCEL_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` read, no direct `openai.com` / Vercel host in any source file. Reaching for `FAL_KEY` or a `fal.run` / `fal.ai` host from anywhere other than the sanctioned connector is the same defect as ad-hoc curl.
   > **Tested by:** `tests/unit/agents-md-invariants.test.ts` ‚Äî `FAL_KEY` + `fal.ai`/`fal.run` hosts are file-scoped-allowlisted to `cli/lib/providers/fal.ts` ONLY (and asserted to actually be read there, so the allowlist isn't vacuous); every other source file is still forbidden, and `VERCEL_API_KEY` / `OPENAI_API_KEY` reads are forbidden everywhere with no allowlist.
2. **`ralphy` is the only entry-point for model calls, ffmpeg recipes, yt-dlp pulls, and project mutations.** Reaching for `bunx tsx` against a TS file, `curl` against any provider API, or `ffmpeg` ad-hoc ‚Üí **STOP**. Either there's a `ralphy` verb for it (check the playbook's `## CLI cookbook` section), or the operation isn't yet covered ‚Äî in which case **propose adding the verb to `cli/commands/`** and stop. Never paste raw API code into a project. The gen-log, asset-manifest, cost rollup, and quality gates all depend on this. **For renders specifically: `ralphy render <id>` is the only path** ‚Äî it expects `<project>/index.html` (HyperFrames). Direct `bunx hyperframes render` is reserved for debugging.
   > **Tested by:** `tests/unit/agents-md-invariants.test.ts` (single top-level `renderCmd` registration; `render.ts` routes through `cli/lib/render/hyperframes`) + `tests/integration/cli-render-from-clip.test.ts` (Remotion-gone).
3. **Reference-required gate.** Fires only for **named real entities the model cannot fabricate** ‚Äî a specific person ("Elon Musk"), a recognizable brand product ("Coca-Cola can", "iPhone 16", "Old Spice bottle"), or a recognizable IP ("Mickey Mouse", "Pikachu"). Generic product / lifestyle work ("my coffee shop's new pastry", "no-name workout app") proceeds **without** refs ‚Äî the default flow does not require them (per `04.02.01`). When the gate fires and no ref is attached, refuse with a concrete ask. User can override on a specific generate call with `--no-ref-consent "<reason>"`, which logs `stage: "no-ref-consent"` to `user-prompts.jsonl`. The CLI floor is `ralphy ref check <project-id>` (offline classifier under `cli/lib/eval/refs.ts ‚Üí needsReference()`); the agent layer adds nuance during intake.
4. **Quality gates refuse, not warn.** If `scoreScenario`/`scoreImage`/`scoreVideo` fail twice in a row, stop and report concrete options. Do not render mp4 over a failed gate.
5. **No auto-launched processes.** No background `hyperframes preview`, no dashboard. Chat is the interface. Use `ralphy doctor` to surface missing keys/deps; `ralphy render <id>` to produce mp4. If the user wants a live preview, instruct them to run `bunx hyperframes preview .ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/projects/<id>` foreground in their own shell.
6. **Always check `MODELS.md`** before any model call. Claude's training is stale.
7. **Always `bun` / `bunx`** (no npm/npx/yarn).
   > **Tested by:** `tests/unit/agents-md-invariants.test.ts` ‚Äî no source file spawns `npm | npx | yarn` as a child process. (User-facing strings recommending `npm update -g @alecs5am/ralphy` to end users are exempt.)
8. **Always `ralphy <command>`** for CRUD, not direct workspace edits.
9. **Speed targets** (`docs/perf-targets.md`): ‚â§8 min cold-start single video, ‚â§25 min 10-batch. Exceeding 50% ‚Äî report before starting.
10. **Templates are the content unit; skills are technical / craft overlays. Read [`docs/skills-vs-templates.md`](docs/skills-vs-templates.md) before routing any "make a video / image / content" request.** Two distinct concepts, two jobs:
    - **Template** = the universal unit of reusable content know-how, organized by media **format** (`video`, `image`, `carousel`, `fb-creative`, `motion-design`, `poster`, `sticker-pack` ‚Äî the `--format` taxonomy is enumerated by `ralphy template suggest --help`) ‚Üí general + style. **This is what the agent matches to a content brief.** Match the format with `ralphy template suggest "<brief>" --format <f>`. A *style* template can also reproduce one concrete video: that is the remix path (`@template:<slug>`, "remix this one", names a slug), user-initiated and never auto-suggested for a generic brief.
    - **Skill** = a technical / operational capability (`researcher`, `evaluator`, `install`, `postmortem`, `templater`), a maintainer tool (`dev-issues`, `dev-loop`, `dev-publish-template`, `dev-release`, `dev-tasks`, `normalize-skills`), or the HyperFrames render-engine skills. Skills are referenced in this system prompt for technical use, **not** as the content-routing default.
    - **Content-niche skills are craft overlays, pending templatization.** The `ugc-*`, `poster`, `carousel`, `fb-creatives`, `analog-horror-psa`, `audio-explainer` skills still carry real craft text. They are being converted to format templates in issue 058. Until then, load them as a **supplement on top of a template/format match** to enrich a brief ‚Äî they no longer drive content routing on their own.
    - **Slugs carry no `ralphy-` prefix.** Audience is marked by the `namespace` frontmatter field (`user` default, `maintainer` for maintainer-only skills).
    - **Where templates live (two tiers).** `ralphy template list` / `suggest` / `use` source from the **public content library** (a static `library.json` served on Bunny CDN ‚Äî the same library the `/library` landing serves, read via `cli/lib/library/client.ts`) and from the **active workspace's templates tier** (`.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/templates/`, user-local, gitignored, flat). Workspace overrides public on id collision; the public tier degrades gracefully when the library CDN is unreachable (workspace-only + a warning). The old repo-public `templates/<category>/<slug>/` folder is retired ‚Äî public templates are matched from the library, not from disk. `ralphy template extract` promotes a finished project into the workspace tier (`.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/templates/<slug>/`); publishing it onward to the public library is the `templater` / `dev-publish-template` path. Format taxonomy: `ralphy template suggest --help` (or the public [`/library`](https://www.alecs5am.com/library)).
    - **The deliverable is a UNIT; blocks are its typed ingredients (#063).** The library/site content model is: a **Unit** (a finished piece, any format) = exactly one **Template** (structure) + one **Style** (look) + N **Recipes** (effects) + M **Assets** (character/location/prop/music). The template/format guidance above is still how you MATCH a brief; this is how outputs are *stored, shown, and reproduced*. Project-side, finished deliverables live in `<project>/units/<slug>/` (formed by `ralphy unit`, #069 ‚Äî COPIES of curated `artifacts/` + a `unit.json` carrying that provenance). Publishing a project's units/blocks to the site library goes `templater` (extract + classify into the 5 entities) ‚Üí `landing/scripts/publish-entity.ts` ‚Üí the committed `library.json` + Bunny CDN media (#056). The graph + adapter live at `landing/lib/library-v2/` (`library.json` is the single source of truth; `index.ts` reads it, the CLI fetches it from Bunny).
12. **Asset catalog before reference picks.** Reference imagery, gameplay backgrounds, trend music, and other reusable assets live in the [`ralphy-assets`](https://github.com/alecs5am/ralphy-assets) companion repo, organized as a **pool** (generic, by kind ‚Äî `italian-brainrot-characters`, `gameplay-loops`, etc.). Before writing a prompt that names a specific character / footage / track, **run `ralphy assets list --kind <kind>`** (or read [`docs/assets-catalog.md`](docs/assets-catalog.md) ‚Äî derived from the live manifest) to see what's already curated. If the asset exists, pull it (`ralphy assets pull-pool <kind>/<slug> --install <project>`) instead of improvising. If it doesn't exist for a real-world entity (e.g. a specific brainrot character not in the pool), **propose adding it to the pool first** rather than generating a drift-prone reference from text alone. Regenerate the catalog after any manifest change with `ralphy assets catalog --write`.
11. **Heavy assets and example projects live in a companion repo** ([`ralphy-assets`](https://github.com/alecs5am/ralphy-assets)). Required template assets auto-pull on `ralphy template use`; cache at `.ralphy/cache/assets/`. SHA-256 verified, no auth needed.
13. **Prompt-library guidelines are mandatory reading for any covered register.** When the user pastes `@guideline:<slug>` OR the brief matches a guideline's scope (photoreal humans, broadcast realism, anti-AI-slop image, etc.), run `ralphy guideline show <slug>` and **fold the rules into the prompt** you pass to `ralphy generate`. Guidelines codify hard-won failure-mode knowledge (skin pores, negative cluster, six-token spine, anatomy gate) ‚Äî skipping them is the same defect class as skipping the playbook read. `ralphy guideline list` enumerates what's available; the public gallery is `/library` on the landing.
    > **Tested by:** `tests/unit/agents-md-invariants.test.ts` ‚Äî `guidelines/` directory is non-empty and every slug has at least one loadable file. (When-to-apply discipline remains doc-only by design.)
14. **Append-only on generations. NEVER delete or overwrite user/agent-produced artifacts without an explicit user request.** This rule applies to *everything* under the project dir `.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/projects/<id>/` ‚Äî `artifacts/<kind>/` (all media kinds incl. `artifacts/refs/`, #105), `units/`, `render/`, `logs/`, `prompts/`, `compositions/`, `prompts.json`, `asset-manifest.json`, `STORYBOARD.md`, `POSTMORTEM.md`, the `postmortem/` directory, `index.html`, and any user-supplied refs ‚Äî and equally to the workspace `shared/` tier (`.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/shared/`). Concretely:
    > **Tested by:** `tests/unit/auto-version-invariant.test.ts` ‚Äî covers `image | video | voiceover | music | sfx | captions | index.html` regen auto-version + `--force-overwrite` escape hatch.
    - **Regen ‚Üí new version, never overwrite.** `ralphy generate ...` on a slot that already has a file auto-writes `.<slot>.v2.<ext>` (then `v3`, `v4`, ‚Ä¶) as of 2026-05-19; the existing file is preserved unchanged. Pass `--force-overwrite` only when the user explicitly asks for legacy destructive behavior. The manifest tracks both; only "promote" a chosen variant on explicit user say-so. **Coverage is uniform across all generate kinds: `image | video | voiceover | music | sfx | captions`** (locked in `tests/unit/auto-version-invariant.test.ts`).
    - **HyperFrames `index.html` is versioned too** ‚Äî before any non-trivial edit to `<project>/index.html`, snapshot the current copy with `ralphy hyperframes save-version <project>` so the prior revision survives at `compositions/v<N>.html`. The `compositions/` directory is append-only; never overwrite an existing `v<N>.html`. Auto-saving on every edit is out of scope (the playbook calls the snapshot before regen / restructure / scene-swap), but the postmortem-diff loop depends on those snapshots existing.
    - **`units/` is append-only too (#069).** A unit (`units/<slug>/` = COPIES of curated `artifacts/` + `unit.json`) is a deliverable, never overwritten. A new slug ‚Üí a new dir; re-`create` on an existing slug ‚Üí `units/<slug>.v2/` (then `.v3`‚Ä¶), the prior unit untouched; `ralphy unit add` appends to `media`, never drops an entry. `ralphy unit` COPIES from `artifacts/` ‚Äî it never moves or mutates the source. `ralphy unit delete <project> <slug>` is the only destructive verb and fires only on explicit user intent.
    - **No `rm`, `fs.rm`, `fs.unlink`, `fs.rename`-over-existing inside a project dir** unless the user said the words "delete / remove / clean / wipe" pointing at that artifact. "Regenerate scene-04" is **not** consent to delete the old scene-04.
    - **`generations.jsonl`, `user-prompts.jsonl`, `user-assets.jsonl` are append-only by definition.** Never truncate, rewrite, or filter them in place. Read-and-rewrite to "tidy" is a defect.
    - **Failed / rejected generations stay on disk** until the user explicitly purges them. The gen-log + manifest reasoning across sessions depends on the failed artifacts still being there.
    - **If the user wants a clean slate**, use `ralphy project delete <id>` (registry-aware) or wait for explicit `rm -rf` permission scoped to a named path. Never volunteer a cleanup.
    - **Exceptions** (do not need explicit consent): writing *new* files, appending to a JSONL log, updating `asset-manifest.json` to point at a new version, and CLI-internal scratch like `.ralphy/cache/` (managed by `ralphy assets clean`). When in doubt ‚Äî keep the file, add a new version, ask.
15. **Site-grounding: when the brief names a brand URL, dispatch a Playwright sub-agent to crawl the site BEFORE drafting any brand-DNA or prompt.** A single `WebFetch` of the home page is NOT enough ‚Äî it misses the documented API surface (curl vs SDK), the real CSS palette, the /docs and /pricing pages, and the actual copy worth reusing. Dispatch via the `Agent` tool with `subagent_type: "general-purpose"` to crawl home + `/docs` + `/pricing` + `/features` + `/examples` + `/blog` (auto-detect from sitemap), screenshot each page, extract computed CSS tokens, enumerate documented API surfaces (`curl` / Python / TS / GUI), and return a structured `artifacts/refs/research.md` digest. Main agent then reads that digest ‚Äî never sketch brand-DNA from memory, never invent SDK ergonomics. Full discipline + sub-agent prompt template at [`docs/playbooks/site-grounding.md`](docs/playbooks/site-grounding.md). Origin: `sotaocr-fb-001` postmortem caught $1.20 wrong-palette burn + 5/32 hallucinated Python SDK creatives, both directly preventable by this step.
16. **Scribe-first when the cut has aligned-to-VO captions.** Don't generate caption SRT or write Remotion / HyperFrames timing constants from imagined word boundaries. Workflow: trim each VO clip to pure speech (reverse-areverse `silenceremove`) ‚Üí stitch into a single track with deliberate gaps via the ffmpeg concat demuxer ‚Üí scribe the stitched result (`ralphy ref transcribe`) ‚Üí derive both scene-cut boundaries AND caption timings from the same word-level `startMs`. Costs $0.003 of scribe; saves the 5‚Äì11 edit iterations the `choose-your-guide-001` postmortem caught. For caption-overlay compositions specifically, run `ralphy generate captions` on each scene VO **before** writing any HF / Remotion timing constants and snap every constant to a word-level `startMs`. Full recipe in [`docs/playbooks/editor/vo-sync.md`](docs/playbooks/editor/vo-sync.md); caption-overlay variant in [`docs/playbooks/editor/captions.md`](docs/playbooks/editor/captions.md).
17. **Background-job file hygiene: do not mutate or delete any file a running background job reads until it completes.** `ralphy generate image --prompt-file` (and any other long-running daemon that consumes prompt / ref / config files) reads its inputs **lazily** during the run, not eagerly at submit time. Deleting or rewriting those files mid-run fails silently ‚Äî the daemon either skips the slot with `--prompt arg missing` or substitutes stale content, with no error surfaced until the postmortem. Concretely: while a background `ralphy generate` is in flight, treat `<project>/prompts/`, `<project>/artifacts/refs/`, the workspace `shared/` files it references, the project's `asset-manifest.json`, and any `--ref` / `--prompt-file` path the job was launched with as **read-only on the user side** until the job exits (`<project>` = `.ralphy/workspaces/<ws>/projects/<id>`). If you need to swap a prompt, kill the job first (`ralphy job cancel <id>` when it exists; until then, `kill` the PID) and relaunch ‚Äî never edit-in-flight. Origin: `ralphy-carousel-001` postmortem #03 caught a `rm prompts/slide-0?.txt` mid-loop that silently broke slides 03-05. The eager-snapshot CLI fix (snapshot prompt-file contents at submit time, or warn-on-delete-of-referenced-file) is tracked separately; this invariant is the agent-side guardrail until that lands.

18. **Auto-capture memory ‚Äî write-and-tell, zero user ceremony (#117).** The user's interface is chat; the CLI is yours. The moment the user corrects you (model pick, register, pacing, style), expresses a durable preference, says "remember this", **or you hit an error and find the fix** (a provider quirk, a flag that behaved unexpectedly, a workaround that held): write it THEN via `ralphy memory note "<rule>" [--workspace] --type <t>` ‚Äî no approval step ‚Äî and tell the user in one chat line: `saved to memory: <slug>`. "Forget that" (in any language) ‚Üí `ralphy memory retire <slug>` immediately. Before writing, `ralphy memory search <key>` ‚Äî an overlapping entry means re-note the SURVIVOR slug (versions up), never a near-duplicate sibling. Do NOT capture: environment-dependent failures (missing key/binary ‚Äî capture the FIX if there was one), negative claims about tools/models ("X is broken" hardens into stale refusals), transient errors a retry solved, or task progress / session narratives (project `logs/` + `postmortem/` territory). `proposed/` staging is for BULK ingestion only (`distill`, `curate` merges) where per-entry chat lines would be noise ‚Äî surface those as a list and let the user say yes once. At session end after ‚â•1 correction, run the light review ‚Äî [`.agents/skills/memory-review/SKILL.md`](.agents/skills/memory-review/SKILL.md); the deep path stays `/postmortem` ‚Üí `ralphy memory distill`. Tier rule: client/universe facts ‚Üí `--workspace`; cross-project craft/model/tooling ‚Üí global. **Re-recall on workspace switch:** `ralphy workspace use` returns the target workspace's memory digest in its output ‚Äî read it before continuing (bare `ralphy` embeds the same digest at step 0).

19. **Research is an opinionated default before the plan (#416).** A low-detail brief, a URL (product / brand / creator), a multi-Unit / content-farm ask, or a platform performance goal auto-triggers research ‚Äî do NOT jump to a generic script. Run the deterministic depth decision `chooseResearchDepth` (`cli/lib/research-bootstrap.ts`): it composes the #412 content-mode `defaultResearchDepth` baseline with the triggers and returns `none` / `quick` / `deep`. Route the depth to the EXISTING surfaces only ‚Äî `quick` ‚Üí the site-grounding sub-agent (#15) / a few `ralphy ref pull`; `deep` ‚Üí `ralphy research run` + `ralphy research scrape-profile`. Never build a new crawl. Distill the result into `ProductBrandFacts` at `<project>/artifacts/refs/research-facts.json`; the production plan's `benchmarkSource` (#407) cites it. The reference gate (#3) is a SEPARATE axis ‚Äî generic product/lifestyle proceeds without user refs even after research; named real entities still gate. Full discipline: [`docs/playbooks/research-bootstrap.md`](docs/playbooks/research-bootstrap.md).

## Routing failure mode

**Missed routing is a defect.** Under-routing ‚Äî skipping the matching playbook / skill and improvising from prompt context ‚Äî is the failure mode this file exists to prevent, on par with skipping the playbook read.

If no row matches: **don't improvise**. Either ask one clarifying question that maps the request to a row, or refuse with the closest in-scope alternative. See `docs/use-cases.md` for canonical utterances.

## On slash-commands

Built-in skills live under `.agents/skills/<slug>/`. Slugs are bare kebab-case (no `ralphy-` prefix); audience is marked by the `namespace` frontmatter field, not the slug:

- **`namespace: user`** (default) ‚Äî user-facing operational + craft skills, e.g. `/researcher`, `/evaluator`, `/workspace-eval`, `/fixer`, `/universe-studio`, `/templater`, `/postmortem`, `/install`, plus the content-niche craft overlays (`/ugc-ad`, `/ugc-unboxing`, `/poster`, `/carousel`, `/fb-creatives`, `/analog-horror-psa`, `/audio-explainer`) that are pending conversion to format templates (issue 058).
- **`namespace: maintainer`** ‚Äî maintainer / dev skills, e.g. `/dev-issues`, `/dev-loop`, `/dev-publish-template`, `/dev-release`, `/dev-tasks`, `/normalize-skills`.
- **HyperFrames skills** (render engine, bare-name, unchanged) ‚Äî `/hyperframes`, `/hyperframes-cli`, `/hyperframes-media`, `/hyperframes-registry`, `/gsap`, `/css-animations`, `/animejs`, `/lottie`, `/three`, `/typegpu`, `/waapi`, `/tailwind`, `/website-to-hyperframes`, `/contribute-catalog`. Installed via `bunx hyperframes skills`.

A skill's body is the source of truth for HOW to execute a flow. The routing table above answers WHICH skill / playbook to use; this file (`AGENTS.md`) is the always-on base context for ralphy work.

---
